heading: Mineral permits and leases
label: 3995 Mineral permits and leases
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to section 565 of the former Appendix to this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

children.heading: Suspension during military service
children.label: a Suspension during military service
children.text: A person holding a permit or lease on the public domain under the Federal mineral leasing laws who enters military service may suspend all operations under the permit or lease for the duration of military service and for 180 days thereafter. The term of the permit or lease shall not run during the period of suspension, nor shall any rental or royalties be charged against the permit or lease during the period of suspension.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: b Notification
children.text: In order to obtain the protection of this section, the permittee or lessee shall, within 180 days after entry into military service, notify the Secretary of the Interior by registered mail of the fact that military service has begun and of the desire to hold the claim under this section.

children.heading: Contract modification
children.label: c Contract modification
children.text: This section shall not be construed to supersede the terms of any contract for operation of a permit or lease.
